entries.name: Michael
entries.description: Michael Jackson’s long-awaited big screen biopic finally lands in theaters this spring, and the stakes couldn’t be higher. As recent big-screen biopics Bob Marley: One Love, Rocketman, and Bohemian Rhapsody reinforced, fans will fill theaters to see their favorite music icons’ stories on the silver screen, and no musician has had a global impact like the King of Pop. Tracing the singer’s meteoric rise to stardom in the Jackson 5 to his unprecedented solo career, Jackson is played by real-life nephew Jaafar Jackson, whose performance dazzled attendees at Cinemacon back in April. Despite the positive buzz, Michael comes with some risk, as Lionsgate and director Antoine Fuqua (Training Day) attempt to give fans a spectacle that honors the star’s legacy while also addressing his real-life controversies. — James

entries.name: Half Man
entries.description: “Uncomfortably erotic” is my favorite two-word take for Richard Gadd's “Baby Reindeer” follow-up. As you might expect, the first reviews are widely divisive for the highly anticipated limited series, which stars Gadd and Jamie Bell as stepbrothers with a complicated 40 year relationship. While the poster gives you an indication of the [uncomfortably erotic] dynamic between these two men, the trailer doesn't spoil the apparent whopper at the midpoint of this six-episode series that wades into the manosphere. — Arno

entries.name: Apex
entries.description: While I’m giving this movie a touch of side-eye for its woman-getting-hunted theme, it’s cute for Taron Egerton to think he’s a match for Charlize Theron in action-movie mode. The former Kingsman plays some sort of bad man who wants to take out Charlize as they both traverse the Australian wilderness. The co-stars did a majority of the stunt work themselves for director Baltasar Kormákur, who has been alternating Hollywood genre films with smaller projects in his native Iceland with varying degrees of success. — Arno

entries.name: Beef
entries.description: I think we’re all going to need a vacation after watching Season 2 of this critically acclaimed Netflix series, which returns with a brand-new, well, beef. This time around, an alarming fight between a country-club manager (Oscar Issac) and his wife (Carey Mulligan) becomes a whole thing after he discovers his newly engaged Gen Z employees have witnessed the blow up. Now, it’s off to the races as both couples vie for the approval of the billionaire club owner (who may have some issues of her own). Series creator Lee Sung Jin says this season’s drama focuses on “the internal repression of rage that you see in the workplace” and an exploration of the generational differences between millennials and their Gen Z counterparts. With an insane cast that includes Youn Yuh-jung, Song Kang-ho, Cailee Spaney, and Charles Melton, in addition to Issac and Mulligan, I can’t wait to watch the conflict unfold from the safety of my couch. — Adrienne

entries.name: Euphoria
entries.description: The long-delayed third season of this award-winning series is finally ready to hit TV screens with its trademark brand of sex, drugs, and high-stakes drama. Set five years after the events of Season 2, the eight-episode arc takes us into the some-what grown-up lives of its ensemble cast. Zendaya returns as Rue, whose past derailed drug deal is coming back to haunt her in what looks to be life-threatening ways. We’ll also catch up with Nate and Cassie, who absolutely should not be getting married but seem to be, as well as Maddy, Lexi, and Jules as they navigate life’s messy twists and turns. Beyond the drama, I’ve always admired “Euphoria” for its cinematic beauty, and Levinson is upping the ante in that regard. A significant portion of the new episodes were filmed on 65mm, making it the first narrative television show to do so. — Adrienne

entries.name: Lee Cronin’s The Mummy
entries.description: Writer-director Lee Cronin has called his mummy flick “one part Poltergeist and one part Seven,” and from what I’ve seen so far, I believe him. This new take on the classic movie monster trades the traditional undead pharaoh for a little girl who, after going missing for eight years, is found trapped in a 3,000-year-old sarcophagus. What she’s been up to and what she even is now is the mystery her parents must solve if they want to save their daughter’s soul. Cronin made his mark with the breakout film Evil Dead Rise, and judging from The Mummy trailer, it looks like we can expect more of the face-ripping, soul-vomitting, skin-flaying, hand-biting, bug-eating, mystery-solving, and close-up-taunting that horror fans loved so much. After all, the real ones know, it’s fun to be dead. — Adrienne

entries.name: Mother Mary
entries.description: Writer-director David Lowery has yet to go fully unhinged with his storytelling and direction, yet it seems like he’s given himself permission with Mother Mary, which heralds the return of Michaela Coel. The “I May Destroy You” creator plays a costume designer with what might be a dark arts/Phantom Thread kind of connection with her estranged friend and client, Anne Hathaway’s pop music icon. Call the corners with three of your friends and hope this is an A24 release that meets the studio’s high bar for mood and meaning. — Arno

entries.name: Margos Got Money Problems
entries.description: I’m no alchemist, but this new Elle Fanning dramedy feels like it has all the right ingredients to make TV gold. Aside from Elle (who we love, obvi), the show sports a whopping A-list cast and was written by David E. Kelly. If you don’t recognize his name, take a minute to scroll his IMDb page. He’s responsible for everything from “Big Little Lies” and “Love & Death” to “Nine Perfect Strangers” and “The Lincoln Lawyer,” so you know you’re going to be in good narrative hands. As for the plot of a young mother turning to Only Fans to make a living? In this economy? I say, get your money, girl. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Boys
entries.description: Expect a “gory, epic, and moist climax” to the series finale, says creator Eric Kripke. Fans of this cynical superhero series get just eight more episodes to see if Butcher and the boys can stop Homelander before he finds the strain of Compound V that will make him immortal. Will Butcher really use the virus to kill all the supes? Is Homelander actually going to rule the world? Are we going to be subjected to more outrageous NSFW situations? Can “Supernatural” fans handle Jared Padalecki and Misha Collins joining the cast for the final season? This show has never played by the rules, so prepare for the worst. Because in “The Boys” there are no heroes. I’m just hoping in the end there are also no kings. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Testaments
entries.description: If there was one thing in “The Handmaid’s Tale” we were all wishing for, it was that June would somehow free her daughter Hannah from Gilead’s clutches. Unfortunately the dystopian theocracy’s hold on June’s first child was too strong, and the series ended without a reunion. This is likely why so many IMDb fans are anticipating “The Testaments,” a spin-off series adapted from Margaret Atwood’s book of the same name, which follows the next generation of young women as they contend with a future of forced servitude. There are some familiar faces to look out for here. Anne Dowd will reprise her wonderfully complex performance as Aunt Lydia, while Chase Infiniti (One Battle After Another) stars as June’s first daughter Hannah, now known as Agnes. Praise be. — Adrienne

entries.name: BTS WORLD TOUR ARIRANG in GOYANG: LIVE VIEWING
entries.description: It’s been four years since BTS embarked on their last large-scale tour, and fans around the globe are itching to see the boys back in action. This month, they’ll get their wish with the first of two live concerts streaming at cinemas around the world. On April 11, BTS will stream their second concert of the ARIRANG world tour in Goyang, South Korea, then on April 18, they’ll return to the silver screen with their show in Tokyo, Japan. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Audacity
entries.description: I've been waiting since Ingrid Goes West for someone to put Billy Magnussen at the top of the call sheet, and I'm biting my nails down to the quick in anticipation to see just how freakishly convincing he is as a tech company CEO in this Silicon Valley takedown, which also serves as ace TV writer Jonathan Glatzer's (“Succession” and “Better Call Saul”) first creator credit. Try not to pass out from happiness when you cruise Magnussen's co-stars. — Arno

entries.name: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
entries.description: Mario and his crew are back, with a new villain (Benny Safdie as Bowser Jr.), a new princess (Brie Larson as Rosalina), and a new sidekick (Donald Glover as Yoshi) in this follow-up to the massively successful Super Mario Bros. Movie, which finds Bowser Jr. following in his father's footsteps as he kidnaps Rosalina, mother of the Lumas. The pitch to kids is pretty straightforward, but if you grew up with a Nintendo controller in your hand, there are enough easter eggs packed into every scene that you’ll swear you found a warp zone back to the decade when Mario and Luigi first imprinted on your impressionable young mind. — James

entries.name: The Drama
entries.description: What would you do if the person you loved admitted to something terrible? What if it was days before your wedding? We’ll all find out together this month when Zendaya and Robert Pattinson star in The Drama, a stress-inducing romantic comedy from Kristoffer Borgli (Dream Scenario). The writer-director was inspired to create the film after reflecting on “the power of love” and the complications that come “when your feelings are at odds with your rationale.” Should we be condemned for our past actions or loved for who we are now? Can you still commit to someone even after you realize they’re actually somebody else? I’m looking forward to watching such talented actors tackle these questions as they struggle to decide if it’s OK to say they don’t know when they’re about to say “I do”? — Adrienne

entries.name: Your Friends and Neighbors
entries.description: Coop! You outmaneuvered your Westmont Village friends and neighbors in Season 1, but a new arrival to town, in the form of an X-Man who is set for a big 2026, is coming for you. Jon Hamm will be doggedly pursued by James Marsden in Season 2 of Apple TV’s crime drama that eats the rich and makes you care about its characters (mostly). The new season rolls out weekly beginning April 3, and the series has been renewed already for Season 3. — Arno

entries.name: Outlander
entries.description: Je suis prest! It's the end of an era, lads and lassies. We're fast approaching the conclusion of Claire and Jamie's epic tale in the final season of “Outlander,” which concludes this May. What's carried me through watching “Outlander” for the last twelve years is the palpable spark between the two stars, Caitriona Balfe and Sam Heughan, whose onscreen love has seen Claire and Jamie through the perils of time travel, separation, war, illness, even death. Whatever happens, we can safely bet that there will be at least one moment of Jamie and Claire touching foreheads and gazing into each other's eyes, which is good enough for me. Though this season represents the end of “Outlander,” in the words of Jamie, “Dinna fash. Nothing is lost, Sassenach. Only changed.” — Tara

node.text: 迈克尔·杰克逊：巨星之路

plot_text.plain_text: The story of the famous musician Michael Jackson, known as the King of Pop.
Ruben turns up at his estranged brother (Niall)'s wedding, acting as a catalyst for a revival of the last 40 years of their relationship.
When an adrenaline junkie sets out to conquer a menacing river, she discovers that nature isn't the only thing out for blood.
A minor conflict spirals into a consuming feud between two individuals, exposing pride, resentment, and hidden pain.
A group of teenagers experience the ups and downs of love and friendship in a world of social networks, sex, drugs, and violence.

node.text: La momie: un film de Lee Cronin
Một Bộ Phim Của Lee Cronin: Xác Ướp
The Mummy: A Lee Cronin Film
ザ・マミー 棺の中の少女

plot_text.plain_text: The young daughter of a journalist disappears into the desert without a trace. Eight years later, the broken family is shocked when she is returned to them, as what should be a joyful reunion turns into a living nightmare.
Long-buried wounds rise to the surface when iconic pop star Mother Mary reunites with her estranged best friend and former costume designer, Sam Anselm, on the eve of her comeback performance.

node.text: Margo e a Falta de Dinheiro

plot_text.plain_text: A young mother struggles to make ends meet. When her estranged father offers help, she starts an OnlyFans account using his advice from pro wrestling. Though successful, she wonders if Internet fame comes at too high a cost.
A group of vigilantes set out to take down corrupt superheroes who abuse their superpowers.

node.text: Los testamentos: De las hijas de Gilead
Os Testamentos: Das Filhas De Gilead

plot_text.plain_text: This sequel to "The Handmaid's Tale" follows Agnes and Daisy, two teens in Gilead who must navigate an elite preparatory school where obedience is brutally enforced. Their bond becomes the catalyst that will transform their lives.

plot_text.plain_text: Showcases their fifth album with honest introspection and universal emotions. The 360-degree stage creates an immersive experience. Join their cultural comeback on screen worldwide.

plot_text.plain_text: In the bubble of Silicon Valley, the unorthodox relationship between a wannabe tech titan and his therapist illustrate the Valley's madness and failings.

plot_text.plain_text: Mario ventures into space, exploring cosmic worlds and tackling galactic challenges far from the familiar Mushroom Kingdom.

node.text: Das Drama - Noch einmal auf Anfang
The Drama - Un segreto è per sempre

plot_text.plain_text: A happily-engaged couple is put to the test when an unexpected turn sends their wedding week off the rails.
A hedge fund manager resorts to burglary after losing his job, targeting wealthy neighbors to maintain his family's lifestyle, but makes a fateful error breaking into the wrong home.

node.text: Outlander: Le Chardon et le Tartan

plot_text.plain_text: Claire Beauchamp Randall, a nurse in World War II, mysteriously goes back in time to Scotland in 1743. There, she meets a dashing Highland warrior and gets drawn into an epic rebellion.
